items.name: moebius-npr-renderer
items.desc: Mœbius-style non-photorealistic renderer for the Practical Course Visual Computing at Stuttgart — a real-time multi-pass OpenGL pipeline reinterpreting 3D scenes in Jean Giraud's comic aesthetic. Quantized toon shading, constant pixel-width stroke rendering, paper-texture overlay; dual-method silhouette extraction (screen-space depth/normal discontinuities vs. mesh adjacency-based front/back-facing tests); temporal coherence via contour polyline grouping and stroke-ID tracking to kill flicker under camera motion.

items.name: amulet-streaming
items.desc: HiWi at VISUS, Universität Stuttgart. Implementing the MPEG video-streaming pipeline for the Amulet project, built on NVIDIA's Falcor framework — NVENC for GPU encode, FFmpeg + Vulkan Video for decode, NVcomp for compression.

items.name: 2dgs-forward-facing
items.desc: Research project on 2D Gaussian Splatting for forward-facing multi-view rendering. Stabilizing 2DGS training under narrow-baseline setups by combining monocular depth priors with hemisphere-gated opacity, addressing depth-collapse failure modes.
